title: Mustardy Vietnamese Fromage Frais Cha-Gio
description: A twist on traditional Vietnamese Cha Gio, this recipe combines the crunch of fried spring rolls with the tangy zip of fromage frais and the subtle bitterness of mustard. Serve as a side dish or add to salads for a refreshing surprise.

ingredients.name: Ground pork
ingredients.notes: lean ground pork, optional: add chopped onion, garlic, and cilantro for extra flavor

ingredients.name: All-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: for dusting the spring roll wrappers

ingredients.name: Mustard
ingredients.notes: whole-grain mustard, or spicy mustard for extra kick

ingredients.name: Soy sauce
ingredients.notes: optional, for added depth of flavor

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine ground pork, chopped onion, garlic, and cilantro (if using).
Add fromage frais, yuzu-cure, grated ginger, mustard, fish sauce, soy sauce, water chestnuts, and cellophane noodles to the bowl. Mix well until everything is fully incorporated.
Lay a spring roll wrapper on a flat surface. Place a tablespoon of the pork mixture in the center of the wrapper.

Brush the edges of the wrapper with a little water, then fold the bottom half of the wrapper up over the filling. Fold in the sides and roll up the wrapper to form a tight cylinder. Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Heat a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add a couple of tablespoons of vegetable oil to the pan and swirl it around to coat the bottom.
When the oil is hot, add a few of the spring rolls to the pan (do not overcrowd). Fry until the spring rolls are golden brown and crispy, about 3-4 minutes on each side.
Drain the spring rolls on a paper towel-lined plate and serve warm, garnished with chopped cilantro and a drizzle of yuzu-cure sauce (see below for sauce recipe).
Mix yuzu juice, rice vinegar, honey, and soy sauce in a small bowl until well combined. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Serve yuzu-cure sauce on the side for dipping.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
